# botyard_credentials (Data Source)

Lists the organization's credentials (non-secret metadata only), exposing each `credential_id` for use with `botyard_bot_credential_assignment`.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# List the organization's credentials (metadata only) and pick the default LLM
# credential's id for a bot credential assignment.
data "botyard_credentials" "all" {}

locals {
  default_llm_credential_id = one([
    for c in data.botyard_credentials.all.credentials :
    c.credential_id if c.scope == "llm" && c.is_default
  ])
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Read-Only

- `credentials` (Attributes List) All credentials in the organization. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--credentials))

<a id="nestedatt--credentials"></a>
### Nested Schema for `credentials`

Read-Only:

- `auth_method` (String) Authentication method — `api_key`, `oauth`, or `none`.
- `available_to_all_bots` (Boolean) Whether the credential is available to every bot in the organization.
- `bot_id` (String) Bot this credential is private to (null for org-level credentials).
- `created_at` (String) Creation timestamp (RFC 3339).
- `credential_id` (String) Unique credential identifier (UUID). Use this as `credential_id` in `botyard_bot_credential_assignment`.
- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether the credential is enabled.
- `is_default` (Boolean) Whether this is the default credential for its scope.
- `key_prefix` (String) First few characters of the API key plus `...`, for identification only (never the full secret). May be null.
- `label` (String) Human-readable label.
- `managed` (Boolean) True when Botyard provisions and funds this credential (managed credits) rather than BYOK.
- `org_id` (String) Owning organization ID.
- `provider` (String) Provider vendor (e.g. `openai`, `anthropic`, `brave`).
- `scope` (String) What the credential is used for — `llm`, `web_search`, `image_gen`, or `integration`.
- `slug` (String) URL-safe credential identifier.
- `updated_at` (String) Last-update timestamp (RFC 3339).
